compiled_state.title: Wire CLI flags through load_config(cli_overrides) — documented CLI precedence layer is unwired
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What
Surfaced by the OSS-best-practices validation of the config epic (a621). The typed-config precedence stack is documented and implemented as CLI > env > project > user > defaults, and `load_config(cli_overrides=...)` / `resolve_with_sources` / `_ordered_layers` fully support a `cli` layer — but NO first-class CLI flag currently routes through `cli_overrides`. So the top layer of the documented precedence is reachable only by library callers, never by the `rebar` CLI, and `rebar config` advertises a `cli` source that the CLI itself can never produce.

This is an incomplete-feature gap, not a correctness bug (precedence is correct when cli_overrides IS passed; library callers can use it today). OSS tools (pip/uv/poetry/ruff) all let CLI flags override env+files.

## Scope / Deliverables
- Pick the config keys that warrant a CLI flag (candidates: --push/--no-pull already exist as ad-hoc flags in EV-1; --threshold in compact; a generic --config-set k=v is an option) and route them into load_config via cli_overrides at the relevant CLI arms, so a flag beats env+file+default.
- OR, if no CLI flags are wanted yet, keep the cli layer as a documented library-only override and adjust `rebar config`'s header/docs so it does not imply a CLI-populated layer that cannot occur.
- Tests asserting CLI flag > env > file precedence end-to-end.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] At least one real CLI flag routes through cli_overrides and demonstrably beats env+file (test), OR the cli layer is explicitly documented as library-only and the transparency header no longer implies otherwise
- [ ] `rebar config` provenance shows `cli` for a flag-set value (if flags are wired)
- [ ] Full non-integration suite green

comments.body: DONE (config-loop, commit 6794eeb1). Wired the documented CLI precedence layer via a git -c style global override: rebar -c SECTION.KEY=VALUE <subcommand> (repeatable, before the subcommand) installs the highest-precedence cli layer for EVERY config consumer this invocation (verify gate, push/pull, display mode, compact, mcp). Implementation: config._CLI_OVERRIDES process-global + set_cli_overrides (clears result cache) + parse_cli_overrides (SECTION.KEY=VALUE -> nested sparse dict, ConfigError on missing = / non-dotted key); load_config + resolve_with_sources use effective_cli = explicit-arg-or-global for BOTH the cache key and resolution (no stale-cache gap; explicit {} opts out of the global); reset_config_cache nulls the global (test isolation; library/MCP never set it). main() parses leading -c/--config/--config= and fails cleanly (exit 1) on malformed before any command runs; only leading tokens consumed (a subcommand’s own -c untouched). AC1 (a real CLI flag routes through cli_overrides + beats env+file): rebar -c sync.push=off beats REBAR_SYNC_PUSH env AND a [sync] config file — tested end-to-end + unit. AC2 (rebar config shows cli provenance): rebar config -c k=v tags the value [cli] — tested. AC3 (full suite green): 1985 passed; ruff check+format clean. Subagent review: ship-ready, no critical/major; precedence/cache correct, verify gate fail-closed on a malformed/bad-value -c, library/MCP isolation confirmed. docs/config.md updated.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

file_impact.reason: rebar -c global override parsing in main()
